rulings.Case Title: MARLA FISHER et al VS. IRMA ENCINAS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANTS IRMA ENCINAS, ANDRES ALVARADO and SAUL ALVARADO's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: continued to August 28, 2014 AT 9:30 am IN dEPT. 501. Moving party and its counsel is again (See CGC-14-536629 7/14/14 minute order) ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than August 5, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SOCHI TA VS. LOUIS KIRTMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted; Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production; Compelling Prodiuction Of Responsive Documents; And For Sanctions Against Sochi Ta
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted; Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production; Compelling Production Of Responsive Documents; And For Sanctions Against Sochi Ta: Moot. Sanctions granted in the amount of $660. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: COSTANZO BUZZELLI et al VS. MARY DEBENEDETTI, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE MARY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT HANFORD-FREUND & CO.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Strike is GRANTED with leave to amend as to paragraph 26, which is incorporated into all causes of action, including contract-based causes of action. Plaintiff does not dispute that punitive damages are not available in breach of contract causes of action. Plaintiff may re-allege this paragraph, if proper, in tort causes of action. Motion is also GRANTED with leave to amend to allege fact regarding punitive damages as to a corporate defendant. Motion is otherwise DENIED, complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support malice, conscious disregard of plaintiff rights and safety. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: COSTANZO BUZZELLI et al VS. MARY DEBENEDETTI, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE MARY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT HANFORD-FREUND & CO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OVERRULED. On a demurrer allegations in the complaint must be taken as true. Complaint alleges that Plaintiffs had a contract with all defendants. Complaint is not uncertain. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN PROPERTIES LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. DENNIS A. ODGERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment After Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 5: PLAINTIFF GOLDEN PROPERTIES' Application For Right To Attach Order; For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment: GRANTED. Plaintiff sustained its burden. No opposition filed. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. GRACIELA ZARAGOZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANTS GRACIELA ZARAGOZA, MARIA VICENTE, ALEJANDRA VICENTE and LUCIANO VICENTE's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT and DEFAULT JUDGMENT and for LEAVE TO DEFEND: GRANTED subject to (1) opposition and (2) Defendants posting $3,716 (rent demanded in the Notice, April, June and July) no later than July 28, 2014 by 4 p.m. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING LLC VS. BARTALOME GOMEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 8: DEFENDANT NANCY SOLIZ's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Demurrer improperly raises issues of fact not appearing in the complaint regarding defendant's tenant rights. (501/AJR)
